tagline: Operate the Miren PaaS from an agent: set up a server, deploy apps, and debug them
description_md: Drive the Miren PaaS from an agent: list and inspect apps, read logs, run diagnostics, and reach any miren subcommand. Structured JSON wherever the CLI offers it.

methods.name: miren.apps
methods.summary: List all applications on the cluster as JSON.

methods.name: miren.app
methods.summary: Inspect one application (status, config, stats) as JSON.

methods.name: miren.logs
methods.summary: Read recent logs for an application as JSON.

methods.name: miren.whoami
methods.summary: Show the current authenticated user and cluster.

methods.name: miren.server.install
methods.summary: Install the miren server as a systemd service on this host.

methods.name: miren.server.status
methods.summary: Show miren server service status (one-shot, no follow).

methods.name: miren.init
methods.summary: Initialize a new application in a directory (writes its config).

methods.name: miren.deploy
methods.summary: Build and deploy the app in a directory (non-interactive: --force).

methods.name: miren.deploy.analyze
methods.summary: Analyze an app's detected stack/services without building or deploying.

methods.name: miren.rollback
methods.summary: Roll an application back to its previous deployed version.

methods.name: miren.debug.connection
methods.summary: Test connectivity and authentication with the miren server.

methods.name: miren.debug.advertise
methods.summary: Show which addresses the server would advertise and why.

methods.name: miren.app.history
methods.summary: Show an application's deployment history as JSON (debug deploys).

methods.name: miren.help
methods.summary: Discovery: every method with params, kind, and latency class.

notes: Initial release: CLI-backed adapter over the local miren binary; structured JSON where the CLI offers it
Inspect: miren.apps, miren.app, miren.logs, miren.doctor, miren.whoami
Deploy lifecycle: miren.init, miren.deploy (non-interactive), miren.deploy.analyze, miren.rollback
Passthrough miren.exec for any other miren subcommand
